title: strychnos dinklagei
intro: Strychnos dinklagei, also known as the African strychnine tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae, native to tropical West Africa. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain the toxic alkaloid strychnine, but also other compounds with potential health benefits. The seeds have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the seeds are often dried and ground into a powder, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or topical applications.
